message:
feat(browser): shared-worker coordinator scaffolding — bounded attach, version handshake, and downgrade

Adds shared-worker coordinator helpers to the browser SDK without
promoting direct-runtime support on SharedWorkerGlobalScope:

Constants:
- BROWSER_SHARED_WORKER_COORDINATOR_CONTRACT_ID
- BROWSER_SHARED_WORKER_COORDINATOR_LANE
- BROWSER_SHARED_WORKER_COORDINATOR_PROTOCOL
- BROWSER_SHARED_WORKER_COORDINATOR_UNSUPPORTED_CODE

Types:
- BrowserSharedWorkerCoordinatorLifecycleState (bootstrapping →
  version_check → attached → denied → lost)
- BrowserSharedWorkerCoordinatorSupportDiagnostics
- BrowserSharedWorkerCoordinatorSelectionResult

Functions:
- detectBrowserSharedWorkerCoordinatorSupport(): probes
  SharedWorker availability without creating one
- createBrowserSharedWorkerCoordinatorSelection(): creates a
  SharedWorker, attaches a per-client MessagePort, performs a
  same-origin protocol/features version handshake, and
  downgrades mechanically to the truthful fallback lane on
  denial or connection loss

Class:
- BrowserSharedWorkerCoordinatorClient: wraps the port with
  lifecycle state tracking, dispose(), and diagnostics

Direct runtime on SharedWorkerGlobalScope remains fail-closed.
The coordinator is only callable from browser main-thread or
dedicated-worker contexts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
